Title: Verify "show all children" checkbox is removed from the Executions table
Pre conditions: User is logged in to SnapLogic Manager and has permission to view executions
Steps (Step):
1. Navigate to Manager > Analyze > Executions
2. Look at the executions table toolbar / filter area
3. Inspect the table options where the checkbox previously existed
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Executions page loads successfully
2. The "show all children" checkbox is NOT present anywhere on the page
3. No leftover label, tooltip, or empty space remains where the checkbox used to be

Title: Verify Children tab appears only when the pipeline has child pipelines
Pre conditions: At least one parent pipeline with child pipelines has executed; one standalone pipeline (no children) has executed
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout for the parent pipeline (with children)
2. Observe the available tabs in the pushout
3. Close pushout and open Run Info pushout for the standalone pipeline (no children)
4. Observe the available tabs
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens for parent pipeline
2. "Children" tab is visible alongside Statistics, Logs, etc.
3. Pushout opens for the standalone pipeline
4. "Children" tab is NOT shown; only the default tabs (Statistics, Logs, etc.) are visible

Title: Verify Children tab lists all immediate child pipeline executions in a flat list
Pre conditions: A parent pipeline with multiple direct children has finished execution
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout for the parent pipeline
2. Click the "Children" tab
3. Verify the list rendered
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens
2. Children tab is selected and shows a flat list of child executions
3. Every direct child of the parent is listed; the parent row itself is filtered out from the results

Title: Verify Children tab columns (Pipeline name, Status, Documents, etc.) populate correctly
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with children has run; children produced output documents
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout > Children tab for the parent pipeline
2. Inspect each column for the listed children
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pipeline name (label) is populated for every row
2. Status column shows correct execution state (Completed/Failed/Stopped/Running)
3. Documents column shows the document count for each child
4. All other configured columns render valid data (no blanks/undefined)

Title: Verify sorting works on the Children tab columns
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with multiple children of varying names, statuses, and document counts
Steps (Step):
1. Open Children tab
2. Click on the Pipeline name column header
3. Click again to reverse sort
4. Repeat for Status and Documents columns
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Tab opens
2. Rows sort ascending by pipeline name
3. Rows sort descending by pipeline name
4. Status and Documents columns sort correctly in both directions; sort indicator is visible on the active column

Title: Verify filtering by pipeline name on the Children tab
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with at least 5 children with distinct names
Steps (Step):
1. Open Children tab
2. Enter a partial pipeline name in the filter input
3. Clear the filter
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Children tab loads
2. List narrows to rows whose pipeline name matches the substring (case-insensitive)
3. Full list is restored when filter is cleared

Title: Verify filtering by Status on the Children tab
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with children in mixed statuses (Completed, Failed, Stopped)
Steps (Step):
1. Open Children tab
2. Apply Status filter = Completed
3. Apply Status filter = Failed
4. Clear filter
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Tab opens
2. Only Completed children are listed
3. Only Failed children are listed
4. All children re-appear when filter is cleared

Title: Verify clicking a child row in the Children tab navigates the pushout to the child
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with at least one child execution
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout for parent
2. Open Children tab
3. Click on any child row
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens
2. Children tab displays the children
3. Pushout content updates to the selected child's Run Info; previous (parent) view is replaced; breadcrumb header now shows the parent as ancestor

Title: Verify breadcrumb is shown in the pushout header when viewing a child pipeline
Pre conditions: Parent and child pipelines have executed
Steps (Step):
1. Drill from parent pushout into child via Children tab
2. Observe the pushout header
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout navigates to child
2. Breadcrumb component is visible at the header showing the full hierarchy path (parent > child); current pipeline segment is shown but not styled as a clickable link

Title: Verify each breadcrumb segment is clickable and navigates to that ancestor
Pre conditions: 3-level pipeline hierarchy (grandparent > parent > child) has executed
Steps (Step):
1. Open child pipeline pushout (drill via Children tab from grandparent)
2. Click on the parent crumb in the breadcrumb
3. Click on the grandparent crumb
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Child pushout shown with full breadcrumb (grandparent > parent > child)
2. Pushout navigates to parent's Run Info; breadcrumb shortens accordingly
3. Pushout navigates to grandparent's Run Info; breadcrumb shows only grandparent

Title: Verify breadcrumb truncation for deeply nested hierarchies (more than 6 ancestors)
Pre conditions: A 7+ level deep pipeline hierarchy has executed
Steps (Step):
1. Drill into the deepest descendant pipeline
2. Observe breadcrumb in pushout header
3. Hover/interact with the truncated ellipsis (…) if applicable
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens for deepest pipeline
2. Breadcrumb shows first 3 ancestors + … (ellipsis) + last 3 ancestors only
3. Truncated/hidden middle segments are accessible via tooltip or expansion (per design); first 3 and last 3 are clickable

Title: Verify Statistics tab parent path is clickable and opens the parent in pushout
Pre conditions: Child pipeline with a parent has executed
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout for the child pipeline directly from executions table
2. Go to Statistics tab
3. Locate the "Parent" / parent path field
4. Click the parent path
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens on Statistics tab
2. Statistics tab displays parent path
3. Parent path is rendered as a clickable link (cursor pointer, link styling)
4. Pushout navigates to the parent's Run Info; breadcrumb updates to reflect the new context

Title: Verify parent path is NOT clickable on the Statistics tab when no parent exists
Pre conditions: Top-level (no parent) pipeline has executed
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout for the top-level pipeline
2. Go to Statistics tab
3. Inspect parent path field
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens
2. Statistics tab loads
3. Parent path is empty / shows placeholder; no clickable link is rendered

Title: Verify X (close) button closes the entire pushout stack and returns to executions table
Pre conditions: User has drilled into a child via Children tab (pushout stack depth >= 2)
Steps (Step):
1. From parent pushout, click into a child via the Children tab
2. From child pushout, click into a grandchild
3. Click the X (close) button at the top of the pushout
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout shows child
2. Pushout shows grandchild with full breadcrumb
3. Entire pushout closes; user returns to the executions table; no residual pushout / overlay is visible

Title: Verify back chevron steps back exactly one level in the pushout stack
Pre conditions: User has drilled into a grandchild via Children tab (stack depth >= 3)
Steps (Step):
1. From parent pushout, drill into child, then drill into grandchild
2. Click the back chevron once
3. Click the back chevron again
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Grandchild pushout is shown
2. Pushout returns to child Run Info; breadcrumb updates accordingly
3. Pushout returns to parent Run Info

Title: Verify clicking a child row in the executions table (no search) shows correct breadcrumb
Pre conditions: Parent + child pipelines exist in the executions table; no search filter applied
Steps (Step):
1. Navigate to executions table
2. Expand parent row to view child rows (or auto-expand)
3. Click directly on the child row in the executions table (not via Children tab)
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Executions table loads
2. Child rows are visible under the parent
3. Run Info pushout opens for child; breadcrumb correctly shows the ancestor chain (parent > child) built from parent_ruuid

Title: Verify clicking a child row in executions table while searching shows correct breadcrumb
Pre conditions: Deeply nested pipeline hierarchy exists
Steps (Step):
1. In executions table, search for a deep child pipeline by name
2. Placeholder row appears; click to expand
3. Click the child row
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Search filter applied
2. Placeholder row expands and shows the matching child
3. Pushout opens for the child with full breadcrumb of ancestors built from the loaded row data

Title: Verify search in executions table does NOT trigger excessive subrow API calls
Pre conditions: Executions table has hundreds of rows including many nested pipelines
Steps (Step):
1. Open browser DevTools > Network tab
2. Filter network calls to runtime/subrow API
3. Type a search query in the executions table search box
4. Wait for results to render
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Network monitoring active
2. Filter applied
3. Search executes
4. Subrow API calls are limited to rows matching hitProperty / nestedPipelineProperty; no spike of 50+ unrelated subrow fetches occurs

Title: Verify expanded rows reset when search query changes
Pre conditions: Executions table loaded; user has expanded several parent rows
Steps (Step):
1. Expand 3 parent rows in the executions table
2. Type a search query
3. Clear the search query
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Rows are expanded
2. Search runs; previously expanded state is reset (stale expansions cleared); only relevant matches are auto-expanded
3. Table returns to default state without orphaned expanded rows

Title: Verify Time Started / Time Completed buttons open Logs tab (not Children tab) for nested pipelines
Pre conditions: Nested (child) pipeline has executed
Steps (Step):
1. In executions table, locate a nested pipeline row
2. Click the Time Started value
3. Close pushout, then click Time Completed value on the same row
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Row is visible
2. Run Info pushout opens directly on the Logs tab (not Children tab)
3. Run Info pushout opens directly on the Logs tab; tab index is correctly shifted to account for the inserted Children tab

Title: Verify Children tab is NOT shown for non-nested pipelines and tab routing remains unchanged
Pre conditions: Standalone pipeline (no children) has executed
Steps (Step):
1. Click the Time Started value of the standalone pipeline row
2. Click the Time Completed value
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens directly on Logs tab; Children tab is absent; tab indexes are not shifted
2. Same behavior — Logs tab is shown directly

Title: Verify loading state is shown while navigating between pipelines in the pushout
Pre conditions: Multi-level pipeline hierarchy with appreciable load latency
Steps (Step):
1. Open parent pushout
2. Click into a child via Children tab
3. Click a breadcrumb to navigate back to parent
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout loads
2. Loading indicator/spinner is visible while child Run Info is being fetched; UI is not blank/broken
3. Loading indicator is shown briefly while parent re-loads

Title: Verify breadcrumb behavior is consistent when navigating Children tab → drill in → back
Pre conditions: 3-level hierarchy: A (grandparent) > B (parent) > C (child)
Steps (Step):
1. Open A's Run Info > Children tab
2. Click B → opens B with breadcrumb A > B
3. Click B's Children tab > click C → opens C with breadcrumb A > B > C
4. Click A in breadcrumb
Steps (Expected Result):
1. A's Children tab shows B
2. Pushout shows B with crumb A > B
3. Pushout shows C with crumb A > B > C
4. Pushout returns to A; breadcrumb collapses to just A; Children tab is selectable again

Title: Verify executions and infrastructure filter states are preserved when navigating across pushouts (regression)
Pre conditions: Filters applied on executions table (e.g., date range, status, project)
Steps (Step):
1. Apply 2-3 filters on the executions table
2. Open a Run Info pushout, drill into Children tab → child → breadcrumb back to parent
3. Close pushout
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Filters applied
2. Pushout navigation works without resetting filters
3. Filters remain applied on the executions table after pushout closes

Title: Verify pushout closes only itself on unmount and does not close unrelated panels (regression)
Pre conditions: Multiple side panels can be opened (e.g., Run Info pushout + another panel)
Steps (Step):
1. Open a non-runInfo side panel
2. Open Run Info pushout for an execution
3. Navigate away (e.g., switch route or close Run Info)
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Other panel is open
2. Both panels visible
3. Only the Run Info pushout closes; the unrelated panel remains open

Title: Verify pushout works for a pipeline with a very large number of children (scalability)
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with 100+ child executions exists
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info pushout for the parent pipeline
2. Open Children tab
3. Scroll through the children list
4. Apply a filter / sort
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens
2. Children tab loads without freezing the UI
3. Scroll/virtualization works smoothly; document count and status render correctly
4. Sort and filter perform within acceptable response time (<2s)

Title: Verify breadcrumb displays correctly when navigating directly to a deeply nested pipeline via URL/deep-link
Pre conditions: Deeply nested pipeline (4+ levels)
Steps (Step):
1. Copy a deep-link to a child pipeline's Run Info pushout
2. Open the URL in a new browser tab
Steps (Expected Result):
1. URL copied
2. Pushout opens directly on the deep child; full ancestor breadcrumb is rendered correctly with all crumbs clickable

Title: Verify Children tab is hidden when child pipelines list becomes empty after a filter clears all rows
Pre conditions: Parent with multiple children
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info > Children tab for parent
2. Apply a pipeline-name filter that matches no children
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Children tab loads with all children
2. Children tab remains selected; an "empty results" message is displayed within the tab (tab itself is NOT removed since the pipeline still has children — only the filtered view is empty)

Title: Verify keyboard accessibility on breadcrumb and Children tab
Pre conditions: User uses keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter)
Steps (Step):
1. Open pushout with breadcrumb
2. Tab through breadcrumb segments
3. Press Enter on a focused crumb
4. Tab into Children tab list and press Enter on a row
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Pushout opens
2. Each crumb is focusable; visible focus ring rendered
3. Pushout navigates to that ancestor
4. Child row activates and pushout navigates to the child

Title: Verify Children tab persists/restores correctly when pushout is reopened on the same execution
Pre conditions: Parent pipeline with children
Steps (Step):
1. Open Run Info > Children tab for the parent
2. Apply a filter
3. Close the pushout (X)
4. Reopen the same parent's Run Info
Steps (Expected Result):
1. Tab opens
2. Filter applied
3. Pushout closes
4. Pushout reopens on the default tab (Statistics or last tab per existing convention); Children tab filter state is reset to default (or restored consistently with other tabs)
